settings.text: Built for barking, pacing, chewing, and panic behaviors that start the moment you step away.

settings.eyebrow: A calmer reset routine for dogs who spiral when you leave
settings.headline: Help your dog settle faster when being left alone triggers the panic spiral.
settings.subtitle: A pheromone-based calming collar designed to reinforce safety cues during the departure moments that set separation anxiety off.
settings.description: CalmiCollar is best framed as a wearable calm-support layer for dogs whose barking, pacing, chewing, or indoor accidents begin when the brain shifts into survival mode.

settings.title: Sedation is not the same as feeling safe
settings.text: Even when a dog seems quieter, that does not always mean the nervous system has actually reset out of panic when you leave.

settings.title: More confinement can intensify the alarm
settings.text: Crating or isolation can make some dogs feel even more trapped when the survival response is already firing.

settings.title: Distraction is not a reset signal
settings.text: Background noise might fill the room, but it does not necessarily tell the brain that the separation event is safe.

settings.title: A tired dog can still be panicking
settings.text: Physical exhaustion does not always stop emotional panic, destructive chewing, or stress barking once the alarm response begins.

settings.title: Separation anxiety starts as a nervous-system alarm, not a behavior problem.
settings.text: When some dogs see keys, shoes, or the door close, the brain can shift straight into survival mode. Barking, pacing, indoor accidents, chewing, and frantic searching are often downstream signs of a dog that no longer feels safe being left alone.
settings.analogy_title: The goal is to reset the alarm signal before panic takes over.
settings.analogy_text: CalmiCollar is best framed as a wearable safety cue that helps the nervous system settle faster, so training and routine have a calmer starting point.

settings.title: CAP = turns down outside alarm cues
settings.text: Helps reduce the intensity of external threat signals that can push departure moments into panic.

settings.title: MAP = reinforces the 'you're safe' cue
settings.text: Mimics a maternal reassurance signal associated with settling, security, and calmer alone-time transitions.

settings.title: Why NeuroEase is built to turn the alarm down before the spiral builds.
settings.text: The collar's role is not to knock a dog out. It is to create steadier safety cues so the nervous system can move from high alert toward a calmer baseline.

settings.title: Faster recovery after departure cues
settings.text: Supports shorter panic spikes after the shoes, keys, crate, or door routine begins.

settings.title: Less barking, whining, and pacing
settings.text: Supports a calmer response when your dog realizes they are being left alone.

settings.title: A calmer baseline for training
settings.text: Creates a steadier starting point so gradual alone-time work and training cues are easier to absorb.

settings.title: Less destructive coping behavior
settings.text: Helps reduce panic behaviors like chewing, scratching, and frantic searching.

settings.title: More realistic alone-time practice
settings.text: Built for dogs who panic when being left alone, even during short routines, and need a calmer starting point.

settings.title: The day-to-day signs owners want to see as survival mode starts easing.
settings.text: CalmiCollar is best positioned around calmer transitions: less immediate panic, faster settling, and a better starting point for training over time.

30-Day Money-Back Guarantee
settings.text: If CalmiCollar is not the right fit for your dog's calm-support routine, we will refund your purchase. No hassles, no questions asked.

settings.question: How long does one collar last?
settings.answer: Each CalmiCollar is designed to last up to 30 days of continuous wear.

settings.question: Will it fit my dog?
settings.answer: Yes. It is one-size-fits-all and can be cut to size for a more comfortable fit.

settings.question: Is the smell strong?
settings.answer: No. The scent is mild with a light lavender profile, not overpowering or unpleasant.

settings.question: Will it make my dog sleepy?
settings.answer: No. CalmiCollar is designed to promote calm, not sedation, so your dog can still stay alert and trainable.

settings.question: Can I use it alongside training?
settings.answer: Yes. It works best as a calm-support layer alongside routine, gradual alone-time practice, and training consistency.
